revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Sebastian | Guid=3005-17224 | Deck=A character who gives players hints and tips while playing ilomilo by SouthEnd Interactive. | Gender=Male | Concepts= | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends=Characters/Ilo,Characters/Milo_15232 | Games=Games/ilomilo | Locations= | People= | Objects= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/7/72276/1642794-ilomilo_sebastian.jpg }}
==Sebastian Facts== * Sebastian owns 73 hats but wears only one. The rest are fill ed with notes on the words he created himself. * He lives in small blocks with a single door and a lamppost on top and flies around on the back of a nameless, ladybug-like creature. * In order to call his "majestic flying stallion" he must whistle through his left nostril three times, a challenging and frequently icky feat as he has many allergies including a reaction to garlic. * Sebastian sometimes vacations in the real world. * He once used his savings to buy the world's largest garlic which he continued making into garlic juice for several years. * Sebastian has an obsession with his physique, in particular his biceps. * He claims to be a "marvelous gentleman with an incredible sense for fashion"  
 Sebastian also tells players stories who reach a number of secret bonus stages. Split into several parts across several stages, he relates the story of "The Huntsman and the Fox", a semi-autobiographical tale depicted in the style of an 8-bit role-playing game.

{{Character | Name=Selena | Guid=3005-58699 | Concepts= | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/Chaos_Zero_Nightmare | Locations= | People= | Objects= }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' />

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Shina | Guid=3005-5771 | Deck=Shina is Gado's adopted daughter and first appeared in Bloody Roar 2. | RealName=Jane Gado | Gender=Non-Binary | Concepts=Concepts/Breast_Bounce,Concepts/Feline_Humanoid,Concepts/Martial_Arts,Concepts/Sexualized_Women,Concepts/Shape_Shifting,Concepts/Tomboy | Enemies=Characters/Busuzima,Characters/Shenlong,Characters/Uranus | Franchises=Franchises/Bloody_Roar | Friends=Characters/Cronos_5750,Characters/Gado | Games=Games/Bloody_Roar_3,Games/Bloody_Roar_4,Games/Bloody_Roar_Extreme,Games/Bloody_Roar_II,Games/Bloody_Roar_Primal_Fury | Locations= | People=People/Atsuko_Tanaka,People/Jennifer_Hale | Objects=Objects/Fist | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/1/14675/503681-shinahumain.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"503681-shinahumain.jpg","path":"1\/14675\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,screen_medium,square_avatar,scale_small,square_tiny,scale_large,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' /> '''full name''': Jeanne Gado / Shina (known as Marvel in Japan)
===Voiced by=== : Lenne Hardt (Bloody Roar 3), Vanessa Marshall (Bloody Roar: Primal Fury/Extreme) & Karen Lee (Bloody Roar 4)
===Age=== : 19 (BR2) 20 (BR3/BR:PF/BR:EX) 21 (BR4)
===Nationality=== : France
===Beast form=== : Leopard
===Height=== : 167cm (170cm in Beast form)
===Weight=== : 60kg (62kg in Beast form)
===Vital statistics=== : 89-59-90
===Occupation=== : Mercenary
===Likes=== : Tea and victory
===Dislikes=== : Coffee, makeup and frilly dresses)
===Ability plus=== : Shina gains the Any Cancel B and Counter Effect abilities.
===Relationships=== : [[Characters/Gado|Gado]] (father), [[Characters/Cronos_5750|Cronos]] (childhood friend)
Shina first appeared in Bloody Roar 2

revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Shroud | Guid=3005-58847 | Deck=An embittered former Superhero turned Supervillain and criminal mastermind. Augments his already high intelligence with a helmet that doubles as cybernetic implants, turning his brain into a living computer that lets him calculate probabilities and anticipate other's moves. The main antagonist of Dispatch | Concepts=Concepts/Betrayal,Concepts/Cybernetics,Concepts/Evil_Organization,Concepts/Hero_Turned_Evil,Concepts/Lawbreaking,Concepts/Multiple_Endings,Concepts/Murder,Concepts/Organized_Crime,Concepts/Sadism,Concepts/Skull_Helmets,Concepts/Sociopath,Concepts/Supervillain,Concepts/Torture | Enemies=Characters/Blonde_Blazer,Characters/Chase_58771,Characters/Coupe,Characters/Flambae,Characters/Golem_58781,Characters/Invisigal,Characters/Malevola,Characters/Phenomaman,Characters/Prism,Characters/Punch_Up,Characters/Robert_Robertson_III,Characters/Sonar,Characters/Waterboy | Franchises= | Friends=Characters/Armstrong,Characters/Coupe,Characters/Sonar,Characters/Toxic | Games=Games/Dispatch | Locations=Locations/California | People= | Objects=Objects/Coat,Objects/Gloves,Objects/Helmet | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/77/776653/3828163-busts_shroud.jpg}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3828163-busts_shroud.jpg","path":"77\/776653\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_small,scale_super,square_medium,scale_medium,square_tiny,scale_small,square_avatar,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,square_mini,scale_large,scale_avatar","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> An embittered former Superhero turned Supervillain and criminal mastermind. Augments his already high intelligence with a helmet that doubles as cybernetic implants, turning his brain into a living computer that lets him calculate probabilities and anticipate other's moves. The main antagonist of Dispatch

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Skeleton | Guid=3005-6804 | Deck=Character for Castle Crashers | Concepts=Concepts/Magic,Concepts/Skeleton_Warriors,Concepts/Undead | Enemies=Characters/Blue_Knight,Characters/Burly_Bear,Characters/Green_Knight,Characters/Orange_Knight,Characters/Red_Knight,Characters/Seahorse_Orb,Characters/Snailburt,Characters/Spiny_Orb,Characters/Weapon_Frog | Franchises= | Friends=Characters/Bear,Characters/Brute,Characters/Killer_Beekeeper,Characters/Necromancer,Characters/Ninja,Characters/Painter,Characters/Royal_Guard,Characters/Saracen,Characters/Skeleton_6804 | Games=Games/Castle_Crashers | Locations= | People=People/Dan_Paladin | Objects=Objects/Bow,Objects/Shield,Objects/Skull,Objects/Sword | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/1/15471/591922-skeleton.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"591922-skeleton.jpg","path":"1\/15471\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_avatar,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_small,square_small,square_mini,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,scale_tiny,scale_super,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' />
==Overview== The Skeleton is an enemy and playable character in the game [[Games/Castle_Crashers|Castle Crashers]]. He is found in multiple levels and is usually raised by the Necromancer.
==Magic== He uses dark magic that takes the form of small black clouds of smoke. '''Splash Attack''': Pillars of smoke raise from the ground. More points = more pillars. Good for attacking enemies that cannot be knocked back. '''Projectile''': Shoots a black cloud of dark magic. '''Air Projectile''': Shoots a downward angled or horizontal ball of dark magic depending on which spell was used. '''Magic Jump''': Propelled into the air by a cloud of dark magic. Damages any nearby enemies.
==How To Obtain== The skeleton is obtained by beating the game with the [[Characters/Bear|Bear]].

{{Character | Name=Skip Hildegard | Guid=3005-58595 | Deck=the localization of Taiki Yamada, who makes his sole appearance in Crash 'N' The Boys: Street Challenge | RealName=Winston Hildegard Jr. | Gender=Male | Birthday=1974-04-01 | Concepts=Concepts/Cowardly_Enemies | Enemies=Characters/Cheese_Romano,Characters/Crash_Cooney,Characters/Dragon_Woo,Characters/Ramon_Garcia,Characters/Todd_Thornley,Characters/Troy_Noise_Jackson | Franchises= | Friends=Characters/Arthur_Van_Smythe,Characters/Johnny_58590,Characters/Todd_Thornley | Games=Games/Crash_N_the_Boys_Street_Challenge,Games/Double_Dragon_And_Kunio_kun_Retro_Brawler_Bundle | Locations=Locations/The_United_States_of_America | People= | Objects= }}

revision.comment: Batch update: Fixed Image URL
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Solis R8000 | Guid=3005-58572 | Deck=Solis is a young police woman from the year 2115. She was assigned to investigate the activities of a powerful organization of which is suspected of changing some events in the past for its convenience; in her investigation she discovered that this organization was responsible for the disappearance of some important families and financial groups 100 years ago; among these the Goketsuji Clan. | Concepts=Concepts/Android,Concepts/Breast_Bounce,Concepts/Bust_Waist_Hip_Measurement,Concepts/Descendants_of_Other_Characters,Concepts/Police,Concepts/Revealing_Attire,Concepts/Sexualized_Women | Enemies=Characters/Bristol_Weller,Characters/Damian_Shade,Characters/Rudolph_Gartheimer | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/Groove_on_Fight_Gouketsuji_Ichizoku_3 | Locations= | People= | Objects=Objects/Gun,Objects/Hat | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/36/361609/3805659-solis_r8000.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3805659-solis_r8000.png","path":"36\/361609\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,scale_small,scale_tiny,square_small,square_medium,square_mini,square_avatar,square_tiny,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,scale_large,scale_avatar","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Solis is a young police woman from the year 2115. She was assigned to investigate the activities of a powerful organization of which is suspected of changing some events in the past for its convenience; in her investigation she discovered that this organization was responsible for the disappearance of some important families and financial groups 100 years ago; among these the Goketsuji Clan.

revision.comment: Batch update: Fixed Image URL
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Sonar | Guid=3005-58775 | Deck=A half-man, half-bat hybrid with a background in Crypto and business related scams and crimes. One of several ex-Supervillains trying to succeed as a member of the Z-Team at the Superhero Dispatch Network. | Concepts= | Enemies=Characters/Armstrong,Characters/Coupe,Characters/Invisigal,Characters/Shroud_58847 | Franchises= | Friends=Characters/Blonde_Blazer,Characters/Chase_58771,Characters/Golem_58781,Characters/Invisigal,Characters/Prism,Characters/Robert_Robertson_III,Characters/Shroud_58847 | Games=Games/Dispatch | Locations= | People= | Objects= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/77/776653/3821248-dossier_sonar_normal.png }}

One of several ex-Supervillains trying to succeed as a member of the Z-Team at the Superhero Dispatch Network.

revision.comment: Batch update: Fixed Image URL
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Sujiroku Tenjinbashi | Guid=3005-58624 | Deck=Sujiroku is the eldest son of the chairman of the Tenjinbashi family, but when his father died, he had to assume the role of leader at a very young age. Sujiroku's family's belongs to the japanese mafia (Mafia has conecction with the Goketsuji Family), however he is not interested in mafia's bussines, he just enjoys their celebration festivals and has participated in many important cultural events all around Japan. | Concepts=Concepts/Clownish_Combatant,Concepts/Cryokinesis,Concepts/Elementals,Concepts/Lightning_Manipulation,Concepts/Musician_Fighter | Enemies=Characters/Bristol_Weller,Characters/Damian_Shade,Characters/Rudolph_Gartheimer | Franchises= | Friends=Characters/Hizumi_Yukinoue | Games=Games/Groove_on_Fight_Gouketsuji_Ichizoku_3 | Locations=Locations/Japan | People= | Objects=Objects/Bomb,Objects/Explosives,Objects/Mask,Objects/Stick | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/36/361609/3810178-sujiroku_tenjinbashi.png}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3810178-sujiroku_tenjinbashi.png","path":"36\/361609\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,scale_small,square_avatar,square_small,square_mini,square_medium,square_tiny,scale_tiny,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,scale_large,scale_avatar","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> Sujiroku is the eldest son of the chairman of the Tenjinbashi family, but when his father died, he had to assume the role of leader at a very young age. Sujiroku's family's belongs to the japanese mafia (Mafia has conecction with the Goketsuji Family), however he is not interested in mafia's bussines, he just enjoys their celebration festivals and has participated in many important cultural events all around Japan.

{{Character | Name=Sunny | Guid=3005-26365 | Concepts= | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/Monopoly_Hotels | Locations= | People= | Objects= }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' />

{{Character | Name=Super Granny | Guid=3005-58601 | Gender=Non-Binary | Concepts=Concepts/Character_Redesign,Concepts/Digging,Concepts/Edge_Wobble,Concepts/Flanderization,Concepts/Flower_Picking,Concepts/Geriatric_Protagonist,Concepts/Human,Concepts/Idle_Animations,Concepts/Idle_Dialogue,Concepts/Player_Character,Concepts/Power_Up,Concepts/Sliding_Down_Ladders,Concepts/Super_Form,Concepts/Super_Mode,Concepts/Swinging,Concepts/Titular_Protagonist,Concepts/Voice_Acting | Enemies=Characters/Colonel_Hans_Foote,Characters/Demon_Mouse,Characters/Dog_58603,Characters/Gnome_58602,Characters/Monkey_43350,Characters/Mr_Fang,Characters/Robo_Kitty,Characters/Robot,Characters/Tiki_Man,Characters/Twinkletoes,Characters/Venus_Horror_Trap | Franchises=Franchises/Super_Granny | Friends=Characters/Hilda_58632,Characters/Kamila_58629,Characters/Killer_Tomato,Characters/Kitty_58604,Characters/Margaret_58630,Characters/Stephen_58641,Characters/Twinkletoes | Games=Games/Super_Granny,Games/Super_Granny_4,Games/Super_Granny_5,Games/Super_Granny_6,Games/Super_Granny_Winter_Wonderland | Locations=Locations/Amusement_Park,Locations/Cave,Locations/Factory,Locations/Farm,Locations/Forest,Locations/Ice_Cave,Locations/Jungle,Locations/Mount_Vertigo,Locations/Pirate_Ship,Locations/Resort,Locations/Splashbucklers,Locations/The_American_FUNtier,Locations/Transylfangia,Locations/Underwater,Locations/Volcano | People=People/Bhama_Roget | Objects=Objects/Baseball_Bat,Objects/Bomb,Objects/Boots,Objects/Cane,Objects/Coat,Objects/Dress,Objects/Earrings,Objects/Fedora_Hat,Objects/Flowers,Objects/Frying_Pan,Objects/Glasses,Objects/Hat,Objects/Jackhammer,Objects/Key,Objects/Lipstick,Objects/Necklace,Objects/Pearl_Necklace,Objects/Purse,Objects/Sandals,Objects/Shield,Objects/Shoes,Objects/Shopping_Bag,Objects/Shovel,Objects/Slime,Objects/Sneakers,Objects/Super_Sneakers,Objects/Torch,Objects/Track_Jacket,Objects/Track_Pants,Objects/Umbrella,Objects/Watering_Can,Objects/Whip,Objects/Whistle }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' />

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Texas | Guid=3005-43107 | Deck=A soft-spoken Lupo from Columbia who works for Penguin Logistics, incredibly skilled at melee combat. | Gender=Non-Binary | Concepts= | Enemies=Characters/Lappland | Franchises= | Friends=Characters/Doctor_43088,Characters/Emperor,Characters/Exusiai,Characters/Lappland | Games=Games/Arknights | Locations= | People= | Objects= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/2/24786/3201409-7071191569-latest }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3201409-7071191569-latest","path":"2\/24786\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_small,square_avatar,scale_super,scale_small,scale_medium,square_mini,square_medium,square_tiny,scale_large,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick,screen_medium,scale_tiny","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
==Backstory== A lupo from Columbia, of the nearly-extinct Texas family, her first name is unknown. What is known is that Texas was a high-ranking enforcer of a mafia family in Siracusa, and had a past relationship with [[Characters/Lappland|Lappland]], before they had a falling out that ended in a brutal fight that Texas won. Sometime after that, Texas turned up in Lungmen, working as a courier for Penguin Logistics, trying to leave her violent past behind. However, Lappland eventually found her again, seeking to bring the "old" Texas back.
==Gameplay== Texas is a Vanguard, of the Pioneer subclass, which means she is relatively cheap to deploy and able to generate up to 12 DP on her own with her skills. Her first skill, Charge γ, auto-generates 12 DP after enough seconds have passed. Her second skill, Sword Rain, not only generates DP, but stuns enemies in a modest range around herself, and deals extra Arts damage on top of that.
==Trivia== * Texas name and likeness both reference the real-life Texas Wolf, a now-extinct subspecies of grey wolf. * Texas usually walks around with a cigarette in her mouth except when attacking. In some regions, the cigarette is changed to a stick of Pocky due to laws about the portrayal of smoking in media. Both items normally cause ''canids'' to get sick, sometimes lethally so, when ingested.

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=The Butcher | Guid=3005-3055 | Deck=The Butcher is the first boss in Diablo, who resides in a small corpse-filled room and wields a large cleaver which he uses to maim and kill his victims. The Butcher utters his famous line, "Ah, fresh meat!" and attacks, wielding his namesake weapon: the Butcher's Cleaver. | Concepts=Concepts/Boss,Concepts/Demon,Concepts/Final_Boss,Concepts/Items_Generated_Via_Permutation,Concepts/Mini_Boss,Concepts/Overlord_5759,Concepts/Satanism | Enemies=Characters/Deckard_Cain,Characters/Demon_Hunter,Characters/Griswold,Characters/Leah,Characters/Wirt_the_Peg_Legged_Boy | Franchises=Franchises/Diablo | Friends=Characters/Baal,Characters/Diablo,Characters/Lazarus_9988,Characters/Mephisto | Games=Games/Diablo,Games/Diablo_III,Games/Diablo_IV,Games/Heroes_of_the_Storm | Locations=Locations/Dungeon,Locations/Hell,Locations/Sanctuary,Locations/Tristram | People= | Objects=Objects/Giant_Axe | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/8/87790/2844955-the-butcher.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2844955-the-butcher.jpg","path":"8\/87790\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_super,square_tiny,square_medium,square_small,scale_tiny,scale_large,square_mini,scale_small,square_avatar,screen_medium,scale_medium,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
===Stats (as of latest patch)=== Resistance: Fire, Lightning
Immunities: None
Life: 110
Damage: 2-20
===Trivia=== * In [[Games/Borderlands|''andlt;spanandgt;Borderlandsandlt;/spanandgt;'']], there's a weapon called the Hyperion Butcher (Unique) which has a description of "Ah...Fresh meat!". This is a random quote from The Butcher when you encounter him. * In [[Games/Warcraft_III_Reign_of_Chaos|''andlt;spanandgt;WarCraft IIIandlt;/spanandgt;'']], there's a monster known as a butcher who also yells, "Fresh meat!". Behind him is a chest containing Wirt's Other Leg, which is a parody of [[Objects/Wirts_Leg|Wirt's Leg]] from ''<span>Diablo</span>''. * In [[Games/Fallout_2|''andlt;spanandgt;Fallout 2andlt;/spanandgt;'']], there's a slaver's guild with a character named Metzger (German for "butcher"). When you enter the guild, you're greeted with the words, "Ah, fresh meat...".

{{Character | Name=The Collector | Guid=3005-30696 | Deck=The Collector is a being as old as time itself. He has an unhealthy obsession with gathering rare items and creatures into his possession. | RealName=Taneleer Tivan | Gender=Male | Concepts=Concepts/Comic_Book_Character,Concepts/Games_Based_on_Comic_Books,Concepts/Supervillain | Enemies=Characters/Daredevil,Characters/Galactus,Characters/White_Fox | Franchises=Franchises/Avengers | Friends=Characters/Sif_17120 | Games=Games/LEGO_Marvel_Super_Heroes_2,Games/LEGO_Marvels_Avengers,Games/Marvel_Avengers_Academy,Games/Marvel_Battle_Lines,Games/Marvel_Contest_of_Champions,Games/Marvel_Snap | Locations= | People=People/James_Gunn,People/Jeff_Glenn_Bennett | Objects=Objects/Infinity_Stones | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/16/164924/2690402-6118651750-32404.jpg }}

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=The Doctor | Guid=3005-4978 | Deck=Time traveling protagonist of the legendary British sci-fi series "Doctor Who". The Doctor is an alien who grew up on the planet Gallifrey who has adventures across time and space. True to the title, the Doctor always fights to protect life and vows never to be cruel or cowardly. | Concepts=Concepts/Alien,Concepts/Alternate_Costume,Concepts/Catch_Phrase,Concepts/Character_Models_Based_on_Actual_People,Concepts/Doctor,Concepts/Female_Protagonists,Concepts/Games_Based_on_Television_Shows,Concepts/Genius_Level_Intellect,Concepts/Last_of_Its_Kind,Concepts/Lego_Minifigure,Concepts/Licensed_Game,Concepts/Male_Protagonists,Concepts/Regenerating_Health,Concepts/Time_Travel | Enemies=Characters/Davros,Characters/The_Master_32444 | Franchises=Franchises/Doctor_Who,Franchises/Doctor_Who_The_Adventure_Games | Friends=Characters/Amy_Pond,Characters/Martha_Jones,Characters/River_Song,Characters/Rory_Williams,Characters/The_Master_32444 | Games=Games/Blood_of_the_Cybermen,Games/Dalek_Attack,Games/Destiny_of_the_Doctors,Games/Doctor_Who_and_the_Mines_of_Terror,Games/Doctor_Who_City_of_the_Daleks,Games/Doctor_Who_Episode_5_The_Gunpowder_Plot,Games/Doctor_Who_Evacuation_Earth,Games/Doctor_Who_Game_Maker,Games/Doctor_Who_Legacy,Games/Doctor_Who_Return_to_Earth,Games/Doctor_Who_Shadows_of_the_Vashta_Nerada,Games/Doctor_Who_TARDIS,Games/Doctor_Who_The_Eternity_Clock,Games/Doctor_Who_The_First_Adventure,Games/Doctor_Who_The_Mazes_of_Time,Games/Doctor_Who_The_Runaway,Games/Doctor_Who_Top_Trumps,Games/Funko_Pop_Blitz,Games/Hugo_II_Whodunit,Games/LEGO_Dimensions | Locations=Locations/Earth,Locations/London,Locations/Mars,Locations/New_York_City,Locations/Space | People=People/David_Tennant,People/Matt_Smith_172052,People/Tom_Baker | Objects=Objects/Jelly_Babies,Objects/Scone,Objects/Sonic_Screwdriver,Objects/The_TARDIS | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/45/453869/3188759-4.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3188759-4.jpg","path":"45\/453869\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_medium,square_tiny,scale_medium,scale_super,square_small,scale_tiny,square_avatar,scale_small,square_mini,scale_avatar,scale_large,screen_kubrick,screen_medium","caption":null},"background":[]}' />
==The Doctor== The Doctor is the main protagonist of the ''Doctor Who ''series and a Time Lord, an alien species capable of traveling through any point in time and space and in doing so became fabled throughout the universe.
===Time Lords===
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369332-gallifrey_landscape_1_.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369332-gallifrey_landscape_1_.jpg|align=right|width=320|caption=Gallifrey}} The Time Lords are humanoid aliens native to the planet of Gallifrey, the Doctor's homeworld. Masters of time and space, the Time Lords are an ancient race who have a non-interference policy in the affairs of the Universe at large (as it happens, this is not a policy the Doctor shares, which has caused friction with his people on a number of occasions). In the classic series, the Doctor explained that Gallifrey was located in the constellation of ''Kasterborous'', and once gave its coordinates as "ten zero eleven zero zero by zero two from galactic zero centre". The Doctor returned to Gallifrey several times during the classic series and had several encounters with his own people, even having a Time Lady as a companion for a time.
In the new series, Gallifrey is lost. During the great Time War between the Time Lords and the [[Concepts/The_Daleks|Daleks]], Gallifrey was locked in a time loop by the Doctor, which results in him being one of the last Time Lords in our universe. Even though the Doctor has not visited Gallifrey in the new series, he has described its beauty on a number of occasions, mentioning twin suns, seas of red grass and silver leafed trees.
While not much is said by the Doctor on the subject of the loss of Gallifrey in the Time War, it is apparent that this loss affected and still affects him greatly, and this is reflected in many of his actions.
===Physiology=== Even though Time Lords are humanoid in appearance, there are a number of signicant differences between Time Lords and humans:
'''Two Hearts'''
The vascular system of a Time Lord is binary in nature, with all the blood vessels leading back to two hearts. Having two hearts has saved the Doctor on a number of occasions, particularly when one heart has either failed or been stopped.
===Respiratory Bypass System=== Time Lords possess the ability to briefly shut down their respiratory system, which allows them to survive the vacuum of space for short periods of time, and even survive gaseous environments or attempted strangulation.
===Telepathy=== During an encounter with several of his previous incarnations, the Doctor demonstrated the ability of his race to read each others' minds. This ability was revisited in the new series in "The Girl in the Fireplace" when the Doctor read the mind of Madame de Pompadour.
===Regeneration=== By far the most significant difference between Time Lords and humans is the ability to regenerate in near-death situations. When a Time Lord does this, he (or she) regenerates every cell in their body to continue living on. However, the Time Lord after regeneration will no longer be the same, as the former appearance and personality will be replaced with completely new ones. According to the classic series, a Time Lord can regenerate twelve times, and thus can have thirteen incarnations. This is a plot device used by the creators of Doctor Who, used usually when the actor portraying The Doctor feels that he can not continue on.
===Age=== One of the mysteries surrounding the Doctor is his/her precise age. Over the course of the ''Doctor Who ''television series, a number of different ages have been revealed. One important factor regarding the Doctor's age is that great periods of time pass off screen between some of his adventures. While in his earlier adventures he was as "young" as 450 years, he was already 750 years old by the time of his fourth incarnation. Allegedly, the Doctor passed 950 years of age sometime in his seventh incarnation.
The discrepancy arises when the Doctor of the current television series talks about his age. The current Doctor (as portrayed by Matt Smith) has stated on screen that he is currently 907 years of age, which seems a bit odd given his previously stated age in the classic series. One possibility is that the Doctor is simply unsure of his exact age, which is quite likely given he is close to 1,000 years old.
===Costumes===
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369805-tom_baker_as_dr_who.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369805-tom_baker_as_dr_who.jpg|align=right|width=320|caption=The Fourth Doctor}} One of the defining features of the Doctor over the years has been his many and varied costumes. One of the more well-known of these was the Fourth Doctor, who had a multi-coloured scarf that was allegedly thirty two feet in length. The Fifth Doctor dressed in cricket attire which had the unusual feature of a piece of celery worn on his lapel (which would change colour in the presence of gasses to which the Doctor is allergic). The Sixth Doctor was famous for wearing a multi-coloured patchwork coat. The Fourth, Fifth, Sixth and Seventh incarnations of the Doctor in the classic series had question marks on some part of their costume (such as shirt collars or umbrella handles or even sweater patterns as seen on the seventh).
In the reboot series, the Doctor has often worn more conventional clothing. The Ninth Doctor would regularly wear a worn black leather jacket, and the Tenth Doctor had an overcoat and several suits he would wear - one blue, one brown. The Eleventh Doctor wears cuffed trousers with boots and suspenders, a tweed jacket and a bow tie.
===Catch Phrases=== The Doctor has always been known for his ability to speak in highly technical terms. However, in recent times the Doctor has taken to adopting several "catch phrases" which he tends to use on a regular basis.
The Ninth Doctor was often heard to utter ''"Fantastic" ''at various points, often accompanied by a wide grin.
The Tenth Doctor would frequently exclaim ''"Allons-y" ''(which is pronounced "Allon-zee") which is French for "Let's go".
The Eleventh Doctor has shouted ''"Geronimo" ''on several occasions.
Also the The Eleventh Doctor has said "bow ties are cool" on several occasions.
===Equipment and Gadgets=== As a Time Lord, the Doctor has a number of advanced pieces of technology at his disposal:
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369808-tardis.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369808-tardis.jpg|align=left|width=298|caption=The TARDIS}} [[Objects/The_TARDIS|'''The TARDIS''']]
The Doctor's spaceship, The TARDIS (an acronym for <u>T</u>ime <u>A</u>nd <u>R</u>elative <u>D</u>imensions <u>I</u>n <u>S</u>pace) has the ability to instantaneously travel through time and space. Despite looking like a 1950s British police box, the interior is vastly bigger than the exterior, and is said to house an infinite amount of three dimensional space.
'''Psychic Paper'''
Since the return of ''Doctor Who ''to television, the Doctor has repeatedly used a piece of "psychic" paper to get out of trouble. In simple terms, the paper displays on it what the viewer wishes to see. He has used it as a form of identification on a number of occasions, and even as a party invitation at least once.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369810-fun_things_to_do_with_the_sonic_screwdriver_prop.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369810-fun_things_to_do_with_the_sonic_screwdriver_prop.jpg|align=right|width=320|caption=Sonic Screwdriver}} '''Sonic Screwdriver '''
One of the iconic gadgets that the Doctor carries around is his Sonic Screwdriver. This sonic device enables him to open locks, but has also demonstrated the ability to overload various technologies (including the Cybermen) and even act as a remote control device for the TARDIS. His screwdriver has been destroyed on several occasions, but the Doctor always replaces it eventually.
==Actors== ''1st Doctor ''
* William Hartnell (1963-1966) ''2nd Doctor ''
* Patrick Troughton (1966 - 1969) ''3rd Doctor ''
* Jon Pertwee (1970 - 1974) ''4th Doctor''
* [[People/Tom_Baker|Tom Baker]] (1974 - 1981) ''5th Doctor ''
* Peter Davison (1981 - 1984) ''6th Doctor ''
* Colin Baker (1984 - 1986) ''7th Doctor ''
* Sylvester McCoy (1987 - 1989) ''8th Doctor ''
* Paul McGann (1996) ''War Doctor''
* John Hurt (2013) ''9th Doctor ''
* [[People/Christopher_Eccleston|Christopher Eccleston]] (2005) ''10th Doctor ''
* [[People/David_Tennant|David Tennant]] (2005 - 2010) 11th Doctor
* [[People/Matt_Smith_172052|Matt Smith]] (2010 - 2013) 12th Doctor
* Peter Capaldi (2013 - 2017) 13th Doctor
* Jodie Whittaker (2017 - present)
==Companions== Although he is a Time Lord, the Doctor is rarely with his own race and frequently travels the universe (usually with a female companion) attempting to right the wrongs of other peoples using his ridiculously large knowledge of science, technology, history, and alien cultures. In recent times, the Doctor has had the following companions:
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369814-billiemos2309_468x340.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369814-billiemos2309_468x340.jpg|align=right|width=320|caption=Rose Tyler}} Rose Tyler
(Billie Piper) was originally a shop assistant in London, until she met the Doctor during the episode ''"Rose" ''and her store was blown up. She accompanied the Doctor during his Ninth and Tenth incarnations. Her boyfriend Mickey and her mother Jackie were often part of her adventures back on Earth.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369817-jack_harkness1.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369817-jack_harkness1.jpg|align=left|width=320}} '''Captain Jack'''
Captain Jack Harkness (John Barrowman) is another of the Ninth Doctor's companions. Like the Doctor, Captain Jack is a time traveller, but from the 51st century. He parted company with the Doctor and Rose when they encountered the [[Concepts/The_Daleks|Daleks]] and Jack died. However, he was subsequently resurrected and did not encounter the Doctor again until late in his Tenth incarnation. Captain Jack is the lead character in the ''Doctor Who ''spin-off ''Torchwood''.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369819-sarahjane460.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369819-sarahjane460.jpg|align=right|width=320}} '''Sarah Jane Smith'''
Sarah Jane Smith (Elisabeth Sladen) is one of the most well-known of the Doctor's companions from the classic series. She was a investigative journalist when she first encountered the Doctor during his Third incarnation, and she continued to travel with the Doctor until they parted company during his Fourth incarnation. She ran into the Tenth Doctor whilst they were both investigating strange goings on at a school in the episode ''"School Reunion" ''Sarah is the lead character in the ''Doctor Who ''spin-off aimed at children called ''The Sarah Jane Adventures ''.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369825-freema_agyeman_medium.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369825-freema_agyeman_medium.jpg|align=left|width=260|caption=Martha Jones}} Martha Jones
(Freema Agyeman) was originally a medical student when she encountered the Tenth Doctor in the episode ''"Smith and Jones"'' She left his company to take on a role in the military organisation UNIT (''UN''ited ''I''ntelligence ''T''askforce), and also a brief role in ''Torchwood''. Martha crossed paths with the Doctor on a number of occasions, particularly during some of his epic confrontations with the [[Concepts/The_Daleks|Daleks]] and the Master. She is also the companion portrayed in the [[Games/Doctor_Who_Top_Trumps|Doctor Who Top Trumps]] game.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369854-donna_noble.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369854-donna_noble.jpg|align=right|width=320|caption=Donna Noble}} '''Donna Noble'''
(Catherine Tate) first encountered the Doctor on her wedding day in ''"The Runaway Bride"'' Following their initial encounter, Donna and the Doctor went their seperate ways. She later tracked down the Doctor again during ''"Partners in Crime" ''and travelled with the Doctor during his Tenth incarnation.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369835-karen_gillan_02.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369835-karen_gillan_02.jpg|align=left|width=320|caption=Amy Pond}} [[Characters/Amy_Pond|'''Amy Pond''']]
(Karen Gillan) is the a companion of the Eleventh Doctor.
==Enemies== The Doctor has travelled throughout space and time, and during his adventures has made many enemies. In fact, some of these enemies have not only featured in the classic series, but have fought the Doctor since ''Doctor Who ''returned to television in 2005.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369843-dalek_076s.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369843-dalek_076s.jpg|align=right|width=320|caption=Dalek}} '''The Daleks''' are the most iconic of these enemies, and have featured not just in the television stories, but also in many of the games based on the show, such as [[Games/Dalek_Attack|Dalek Attack]]. The Daleks were involved in the Time War with the Time Lords, and were also considered destroyed. However despite this there have been numerous appearances of Daleks who escaped the war, such as the ''Cult of Skaro''.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369849-cybermen.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369849-cybermen.jpg|align=left|width=320|caption=Cyberman}} '''The Cybermen '''are a race of humanoid cybernetic creatures that were encountered by the Doctor in his very first incarnation in ''"The Tenth Planet"''. The Cybermen of the classic series originated from Earth's twin planet Mondas, which lost its orbit in our Solar system and drifted through space. To survive, the inhabitants of this world gradually replaced more and more of themselves with cybernetic parts, eventually creating the fearsome metal men. In the new series, the Cybermen were reborn in a parallel universe in the episode ''"Rise of the Cybermen" i''n this story, a dying millionaire sought to preserve his mind by encasing it within a metal shell, and in doing so created a new race of Cybermen.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_medium/2/28430/1369851-master_parliament.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369851-master_parliament.jpg|align=right|width=365|caption=The Master}} '''The Master '''is a Time Lord like the Doctor. The main difference between the two is that while the Doctor strives to right wrongs, and preserve the greater good, the Master seeks only power. The Doctor has encountered the Master many times during his incarnations, and like the Doctor he escaped the destruction of the Time Lords during the Time Wars. He is as brilliant as the Doctor, and is one of his worthiest foes. Like the Doctor, he has been portrayed by many actors over the years. He also featured in the game [[Games/Destiny_of_the_Doctors|Destiny of the Doctors]], where there he was portrayed by Anthony Ainley.
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/2/28430/1369852-new_davros.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/2/28430/1369852-new_davros.jpg|align=left|width=300|caption=Davros}} '''Davros''' was the creator of the [[Concepts/The_Daleks|Daleks]]. Like the Master, he is a twisted and brilliant genius. However, he is more evil and in recent times has sought nothing less than the destruction of all reality. Even though Davros created the Daleks, they have often turned against their creator, and Davros has often been held prisoner by his own "children". Davros has also crossed over into gaming, and plays the role of the end boss in [[Games/Dalek_Attack|Dalek Attack]].
==Potential Future in Games== The British Broadcasting Corporation, the proprietor of the Doctor Who IP, is currently in the process of courting studios for the development of a current gen Doctor Who video game.
